title: LangGraph
oneLiner: Framework for building long-running, stateful agents with controllable workflows.
summary: LangGraph is an open-source framework for building stateful, durable agent workflows where developers need explicit control over graphs, state, checkpoints, and human-in-the-loop behavior.
whyItMatters: LangGraph matters because many serious agents are not simple prompt chains. They need state, branching, recovery, and clear control over what happens between model calls and tool calls.
bestFor: Teams that need controllable workflows, checkpoints, and human review
Builders already using LangChain or nearby model/tool abstractions
notFor: Users who want a fully managed consumer product with no setup work
Teams that cannot review the linked source, license, and operational requirements before adoption
seoTitle: LangGraph: Agents resource for open AI builders
seoDescription: LangGraph profile on OpenAgent.bot: what it is, when to use it, official links, open-source status, and structured resource data.
shareDescription: Framework for building long-running, stateful agents with controllable workflows.

coreStrengths.title: Stateful graph architecture
coreStrengths.description: LangGraph models agent flows as graphs with explicit state transitions.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: This gives developers more control than a hidden loop inside a generic agent runner.

coreStrengths.title: Durable and interruptible workflows
coreStrengths.description: Its design supports checkpointing, human review, and continuation.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Long-running agents need to pause, resume, and recover safely.

coreStrengths.title: Large ecosystem fit
coreStrengths.description: LangGraph connects into the LangChain ecosystem and common model/tool integrations.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: A framework is easier to adopt when surrounding integrations already exist.

useCaseNotes.title: Research agents
useCaseNotes.description: Coordinate search, reading, note-taking, and synthesis across repeated steps.

useCaseNotes.title: Coding agents
useCaseNotes.description: Represent planning, editing, testing, and review as controlled workflow nodes.

useCaseNotes.title: Human-in-the-loop systems
useCaseNotes.description: Insert approvals or review steps before sensitive actions proceed.

compareNotes.title: Choose LangGraph when control matters
compareNotes.summary: LangGraph is most useful when you need explicit workflow structure rather than a black-box loop.

commandLine.label: Install LangGraph
commandLine.description: Use the official docs for runtime-specific setup and examples.

faq.answer: LangGraph is listed with MIT based on its official source links. Always re-check the repository or model card before production use.
